incentive.statement: Direct, Causative, and Applicative must share one recognized-input contract, while source-specific Andrews authorization remains typed and an unknown derivation can never authorize a Direct result.

coordinator.responsibility: Consolidate the derivation-type protocol boundary without flattening contextual Andrews operation rules or unrelated derivation taxonomies.

outcome: One core derivation-type vocabulary now governs engine recognition, application intent, general UI state, URL restoration, shell/Canvas parity, and hostile verification; unknown application, surface, and URL input fails closed.

responsibilities.statement: Own the canonical derivation-type vocabulary, recognition frame, control parity audit, and source-specific contextual authorization boundary.

actors.contribution: Exposed the canonical Direct/Causative/Applicative contract and registered its vocabulary, selection, and non-authorizing parity frames.

responsibilities.statement: Retain unrecognized derivation intent diagnostically and block before a safe Direct layout fallback can become generated authority.

actors.contribution: Reused the core vocabulary and added the missing fail-closed application branch for unknown derivation intent.

responsibilities.statement: Make surface normalization, general UI state, shell/Canvas records, and URL restoration consume or validate against the canonical contract.

actors.contribution: Derived DERIVATION_TYPE and URL/surface recognition from the core contract, blocked invalid URL application, and validated live button-to-Canvas-tag parity.

responsibilities.statement: Deliver the changed module graph together and prove valid, invalid, restored, and poisoned derivation inputs.

actors.contribution: Added unknown-application, unknown-surface, shell/ledger poison, and unknown-URL proof; delivered entry 049 and completed live verification.

responsibilities.statement: Record the bounded slice and report the remaining comparison risks without claiming unrelated worktree paths.

actors.contribution: Recorded scope, evidence, contextual exclusions, and the required end-of-job risk reminder.

contracts.invariant: Only Direct, Causative, or Applicative can enter contextual Andrews authorization; safe layout fallbacks and evidence metadata never authorize an unrecognized request.

retirements.removalEvidence: Application, surface, and URL hostile tests block fabricated derivation input, and live invalid URL restoration leaves the source unapplied with no formula.

closure.detail: Unknown derivation intent can no longer become an authorized Direct result, and repeated shell, renderer, URL, and test vocabularies no longer decide membership.

evidence.detail: Application passed 31/31; Lesson 11 plus continuity passed 24/24; matched UI passed 407/407; grammar-contract registry passed 43/43; syntax, runtime manifest 75/75, JSON, and diff checks passed.

Entry 049 reported authorized direct|causative|applicative button/Canvas parity; valid nemi Direct generated #ni-0(nemi)0+0-0#; fabricated URL derivation left Source unapplied, status blocked, formula empty, and logs clean.

All architecture metrics matched baseline, including unregistered contracts 200 versus 200. The command remains red for inherited live-board size and cross-action dirty-worktree overlap; no path attributable to this slice is uncovered within its own manifest.
